paracetamol.uses: Fever and mild to moderate pain (headache, body ache).
paracetamol.side_effects: Rare at normal doses; overdose seriously damages the liver.
paracetamol.caution: Max 3g/day for adults; avoid combining multiple paracetamol products or alcohol.

ibuprofen.side_effects: Stomach upset, acidity; ulcer/bleeding risk with prolonged use.
ibuprofen.caution: Take with food. Avoid in kidney disease, ulcers, or with blood thinners.

diclofenac.side_effects: Acidity, stomach pain; kidney strain and BP rise with long use.
diclofenac.caution: Take with food; avoid long unsupervised courses.

aceclofenac.side_effects: Similar to diclofenac: gastric upset, kidney strain.
aceclofenac.caution: Take with food; avoid with other NSAIDs.

naproxen.caution: Take with food; caution in heart or kidney disease.

nimesulide.uses: Short-term relief of acute pain and fever.
nimesulide.side_effects: Liver enzyme rise in some users.
nimesulide.caution: Short courses only; avoid in children under 12 and liver disease.

etoricoxib.uses: Arthritis and joint pain (COX-2 NSAID, gentler on stomach).
etoricoxib.side_effects: BP rise, ankle swelling in some users.
etoricoxib.caution: Caution in heart disease and uncontrolled BP.

tramadol.uses: Moderate to severe pain when simple painkillers fail.
tramadol.side_effects: Nausea, dizziness, drowsiness, constipation; dependence with long use.
tramadol.caution: Avoid alcohol and sedatives; serotonin syndrome risk with antidepressants.

aspirin.uses: Low dose: prevents heart attack/stroke clots. Higher dose: pain/fever.
aspirin.caution: Never stop cardiac aspirin without doctor advice; avoid in children with fever.

amoxicillin.uses: Bacterial infections: throat, ear, sinus, chest, urinary.
amoxicillin.side_effects: Diarrhoea, rash; allergy in penicillin-sensitive people.
amoxicillin.caution: Complete the full course; tell the doctor about penicillin allergy.

clavulanic acid.uses: Combined with amoxicillin to defeat resistant bacteria.
clavulanic acid.side_effects: Adds to diarrhoea and stomach upset.
clavulanic acid.caution: Take at the start of a meal to reduce stomach upset.

azithromycin.uses: Respiratory, throat, skin and some stomach infections.
azithromycin.side_effects: Stomach upset; rarely heart rhythm effects (QT).
azithromycin.caution: Caution with other QT-prolonging drugs; complete the course.

ciprofloxacin.uses: Urinary, GI and some respiratory bacterial infections.
ciprofloxacin.caution: Avoid dairy/calcium/iron within 2h of the dose; hydrate well.

ofloxacin.uses: GI and urinary infections; often paired with ornidazole for diarrhoea.
ofloxacin.caution: Avoid unnecessary use for simple diarrhoea; complete the course.

levofloxacin.uses: Chest, sinus and urinary bacterial infections.
levofloxacin.caution: Separate from calcium/iron; caution in elderly.

cefixime.uses: Throat, ear, urinary and typhoid infections (oral cephalosporin).

cefuroxime.uses: Respiratory, ENT, skin and urinary infections.
cefuroxime.caution: Take with food for better absorption.

cephalexin.caution: Tell your doctor about penicillin/cephalosporin allergy.

doxycycline.uses: Acne, respiratory infections, typhus, some tropical fevers.
doxycycline.side_effects: Sun sensitivity, stomach upset, food-pipe irritation.
doxycycline.caution: Take with a full glass of water, stay upright 30 min; avoid antacids/iron within 2–3h; not in pregnancy.

metronidazole.uses: Amoebiasis, giardia, dental and anaerobic infections.
metronidazole.caution: Strictly no alcohol during and 48h after the course.

clarithromycin.side_effects: Taste disturbance, stomach upset; interacts with many drugs.
clarithromycin.caution: Tell the doctor every other medicine you take — many serious interactions.

linezolid.side_effects: Low blood counts on longer courses; BP rise with certain foods.

omeprazole.side_effects: Headache, loose stools; low B12/magnesium on very long use.
omeprazole.caution: Take 30–60 min before breakfast; review long-term need with the doctor.

pantoprazole.caution: Take before the first meal; preferred PPI with clopidogrel.

ranitidine.caution: Was withdrawn in several countries over NDMA impurity — ask your pharmacist for current status.

domperidone.uses: Nausea, vomiting, bloating; improves stomach emptying.
domperidone.side_effects: Dry mouth; rare heart rhythm effects at high dose.
domperidone.caution: Avoid with QT-prolonging drugs like some antifungals/antibiotics.

dicyclomine.caution: Avoid in glaucoma and urinary retention.

drotaverine.caution: See a doctor if pain is severe or persistent.

lactulose.caution: Takes 1–2 days to act; drink enough water.

bisacodyl.caution: Occasional use only; swallow whole, not with milk/antacids.

rifaximin.uses: IBS-related bloating, traveller's diarrhoea, liver-related confusion.
rifaximin.caution: Course as prescribed; not for ordinary diarrhoea.

ursodeoxycholic acid.uses: Certain gallstones and cholestatic liver conditions.
ursodeoxycholic acid.caution: Needs months of regular use; take with food.

metformin.side_effects: Stomach upset and loose stools initially; B12 drop long-term.
metformin.caution: Take with meals; the doctor may pause it around contrast scans or severe illness.

glimepiride.caution: Don't skip meals after taking; keep sugar handy for hypos.

sitagliptin.side_effects: Generally well tolerated; rare joint pain.

vildagliptin.caution: Periodic liver tests may be advised.

empagliflozin.uses: Type 2 diabetes; also protects heart and kidneys.
empagliflozin.caution: Maintain hygiene and hydration; pause during severe illness/fasting.

dapagliflozin.uses: Type 2 diabetes, heart failure, kidney protection.

pioglitazone.caution: Avoid in heart failure; report blood in urine.

telmisartan.uses: High blood pressure; kidney protection in diabetics (ARB).
telmisartan.caution: Don't combine with ACE inhibitors; periodic kidney/potassium tests.

olmesartan.side_effects: Dizziness; rare severe diarrhoea on long use.

amlodipine.uses: High blood pressure, angina (calcium channel blocker).
amlodipine.caution: Don't stop abruptly; report marked ankle swelling.

cilnidipine.uses: High blood pressure (newer CCB, less ankle swelling).
cilnidipine.caution: Take regularly at the same time.

metoprolol.uses: BP, angina, heart rate control, after heart attack (beta blocker).
metoprolol.caution: Never stop suddenly — taper under medical advice; caution in asthma.

atenolol.caution: Do not stop abruptly; caution in asthma/diabetes.

bisoprolol.uses: Heart failure, BP, heart rate (cardio-selective beta blocker).

nebivolol.uses: High BP (beta blocker with vessel relaxation).

ramipril.uses: BP, heart failure, kidney protection (ACE inhibitor).
ramipril.caution: Report persistent cough; avoid combining with ARBs; monitor kidney function.

furosemide.uses: Fluid overload (heart failure, swelling) — loop diuretic.
furosemide.caution: Take in the morning; periodic electrolyte tests.

spironolactone.uses: Heart failure, resistant BP, fluid overload (potassium-sparing).
spironolactone.caution: Avoid potassium supplements; blood tests needed with ACE/ARB combos.

isosorbide dinitrate.caution: NEVER combine with Viagra-type (PDE5) drugs — dangerous BP crash.

isosorbide mononitrate.caution: Same PDE5 warning as other nitrates.

digoxin.uses: Heart failure and atrial fibrillation rate control.
digoxin.side_effects: Nausea, vision changes and arrhythmia at toxic levels.
digoxin.caution: Narrow safety margin — never double doses; levels monitored.

amiodarone.side_effects: Thyroid, liver, lung and eye effects on long use.
amiodarone.caution: Regular monitoring; interacts with many drugs including statins and digoxin.

ivabradine.uses: Lowers heart rate in heart failure/angina.

atorvastatin.uses: Lowers cholesterol; prevents heart attack and stroke.
atorvastatin.side_effects: Muscle aches occasionally; rare liver enzyme rise.
atorvastatin.caution: Report unexplained muscle pain/dark urine; caution with clarithromycin/antifungals.

fenofibrate.side_effects: Stomach upset; muscle injury risk with statins.
fenofibrate.caution: Report muscle pain when combined with a statin.

clopidogrel.uses: Prevents clots after heart attack, stents, stroke (antiplatelet).
clopidogrel.caution: Never stop before the cardiologist says so; pantoprazole preferred over omeprazole for acidity.

warfarin.uses: Prevents dangerous clots (mechanical valves, AF, DVT).
warfarin.side_effects: Bleeding — needs regular INR blood tests.
warfarin.caution: Huge interaction list (antibiotics, painkillers, leafy-vegetable vitamin K); report black stools immediately.

rivaroxaban.caution: Take the 15/20mg doses with food; don't miss doses.

apixaban.uses: Clot prevention in AF, DVT treatment.

cetirizine.caution: See how it affects you before driving.

fexofenadine.caution: Avoid taking with fruit juice (reduces absorption).

montelukast.caution: Report mood or behaviour changes, especially in children.

salbutamol.caution: Needing it very frequently means the asthma plan needs review.

budesonide.uses: Inhaled steroid — prevents asthma attacks; controls inflammation.
budesonide.caution: Rinse mouth after every inhaled dose; don't stop when you feel fine.

formoterol.uses: Long-acting bronchodilator, paired with inhaled steroid.
formoterol.caution: Never use alone for asthma — always with a steroid inhaler.

theophylline.caution: Narrow safety margin; ciprofloxacin and some drugs raise its level sharply.

ambroxol.uses: Thins chest mucus in productive cough.
ambroxol.caution: See a doctor if cough persists >2 weeks.

dextromethorphan.caution: Not for wet/productive cough; avoid with antidepressants (serotonin risk).

levothyroxine.side_effects: Palpitations/weight loss if dose too high.
levothyroxine.caution: Empty stomach, 30–60 min before breakfast; separate from calcium/iron by 4h; regular TSH tests.

carbimazole.side_effects: Rash; rarely dangerous drop in white cells.

escitalopram.side_effects: Initial nausea, sleep changes, sexual side effects.
escitalopram.caution: Takes 2–4 weeks to work; don't stop abruptly; bleeding risk with NSAIDs.

venlafaxine.side_effects: Nausea, BP rise at higher doses.
venlafaxine.caution: Taper slowly — abrupt stops cause withdrawal.

alprazolam.side_effects: Drowsiness, memory effects; dependence develops quickly.
alprazolam.caution: Short-term only; never with alcohol/opioids; taper to stop.

zolpidem.caution: Take only when a full night's sleep is possible; never with other sedatives.

phenytoin.side_effects: Gum overgrowth, unsteadiness at high levels.
phenytoin.caution: Narrow margin — levels monitored; many interactions.

carbamazepine.side_effects: Dizziness, double vision; rare serious rash.
carbamazepine.caution: Report any rash immediately; interacts with many drugs.

valproate.caution: Avoid in pregnancy (birth defects); liver monitoring.

lamotrigine.side_effects: Rash — rarely serious if dose escalated too fast.
lamotrigine.caution: Follow the slow dose build-up exactly; report rash.

sumatriptan.caution: Not for heart patients; max doses per day/week apply.

flunarizine.caution: Long-term use may cause tremor in elderly.

betahistine.caution: Caution in asthma and peptic ulcer.

prochlorperazine.side_effects: Drowsiness; movement side effects on prolonged use.

prednisolone.uses: Steroid — asthma flares, allergies, autoimmune conditions.
prednisolone.side_effects: Increased appetite, mood change, sugar rise; many more with long use.
prednisolone.caution: Never stop a long course abruptly — must taper; take with food.

dexamethasone.uses: Potent steroid — severe inflammation, some COVID protocols.

methotrexate.side_effects: Mouth ulcers, nausea, blood count drops.
methotrexate.caution: WEEKLY, never daily — daily dosing is dangerous; avoid NSAIDs/alcohol; folic acid usually co-prescribed.

hydroxychloroquine.side_effects: Stomach upset; rare eye (retina) effects long-term.
hydroxychloroquine.caution: Annual eye checks on long-term use.

allopurinol.side_effects: Rash — stop and report if it appears.
allopurinol.caution: Don't start/stop during an acute attack; drink plenty of water.

febuxostat.side_effects: Liver enzyme rise; possible cardiac risk debate.
febuxostat.caution: Take regularly, not just during attacks.

colchicine.side_effects: Diarrhoea is the classic dose-limit signal.
colchicine.caution: Never exceed prescribed dose; fatal interactions with clarithromycin.

fluconazole.caution: Interacts with warfarin, statins, domperidone and more.

itraconazole.caution: Take with food/acidic drink; many interactions.

griseofulvin.caution: Take with fatty food; long courses needed.

clotrimazole.caution: Continue 2 weeks after the rash clears.

luliconazole.caution: Complete the course to prevent recurrence.

permethrin.caution: Treat all household contacts; wash bedding.

albendazole.caution: Family members often treated together; repeat dose sometimes needed.

ivermectin.caution: Take on empty stomach with water.

acyclovir.caution: Start as early as possible in an outbreak; hydrate well.

sildenafil.caution: NEVER with nitrates (heart medicines) — dangerous BP crash; heart patients need clearance.

tamsulosin.caution: Take after the same meal daily; caution on standing up.

finasteride.caution: Pregnant women should not handle crushed tablets; takes months to act.

nitrofurantoin.caution: Take with food; not effective in kidney failure.

calcium carbonate.caution: Separate from thyroid medicine and certain antibiotics by hours.

vitamin d3.caution: Weekly 60K sachets are NOT daily doses — follow the schedule.

iron.caution: Vitamin C helps absorption; separate from tea/coffee/calcium/thyroxine.

folic acid.caution: Start before conception when planning pregnancy.

methylcobalamin.caution: Persistent numbness needs evaluation, not just supplements.

multivitamin.caution: Not a substitute for treating a specific deficiency.

b-complex.caution: Address the underlying cause of deficiency.

vitamin e.caution: High doses may increase bleeding tendency.

vitamin c.uses: Vitamin C deficiency, immunity support, iron absorption.
vitamin c.side_effects: GI upset at very high doses.
vitamin c.caution: Kidney stone formers should avoid mega-doses.

dydrogesterone.uses: Progesterone support in pregnancy, cycle disorders.
dydrogesterone.caution: Use exactly as the gynaecologist prescribes.

progesterone.uses: Hormone support in pregnancy/IVF, cycle regulation.
progesterone.caution: Night dosing often advised for drowsiness.

norethisterone.caution: Not a contraceptive at period-delay doses.

clomiphene.side_effects: Hot flushes, mood swings; multiple pregnancy chance.
clomiphene.caution: Cycle monitoring by the fertility doctor.

ethinylestradiol.caution: Clot risk — avoid in smokers over 35 and migraine with aura.

levonorgestrel.caution: Emergency pill is backup, not routine contraception.

betamethasone.side_effects: Skin thinning with prolonged cream use.
betamethasone.caution: Don't use steroid creams on the face or long-term without advice.

tretinoin.caution: Night use only, sunscreen daily; avoid in pregnancy.

chlorpheniramine.caution: Common in combination cold remedies — avoid doubling up.

diphenhydramine.caution: Avoid driving; not for regular sleep use.

phenylephrine.caution: Caution in hypertension and heart disease.

promethazine.caution: Avoid driving; not for children under 2.

levodopa.caution: Strict timing matters; don't stop abruptly; separate from high-protein meals.

carbidopa.uses: Boosts levodopa's effect and reduces its nausea.

trihexyphenidyl.side_effects: Dry mouth, blurred vision, confusion in elderly.

donepezil.caution: Evening dose; cardiology caution with heart-rate drugs.

ornidazole.uses: Anaerobic and protozoal infections (often with ofloxacin).

serratiopeptidase.caution: Evidence base is weak; avoid with blood thinners.
